ci: add mypy config and enable check (#896)

- Add [tool.mypy] to pyproject.toml — ported from roboflow/supervision (strict=true, ignore_missing_imports=false, python_version="3.10", mypy_path="src"); add per-module ignore_missing_imports overrides for torchvision/pycocotools/timm/einops; add ignore_errors overrides for 54 pre-existing failing modules
- Add mirrors-mypy v1.19.1 hook to .pre-commit-config.yaml (after ruff, with --ignore-missing-imports for isolated env)
- Add .github/workflows/ci-typing.yml — runs mypy src/rfdetr/ on push/PR with CPU-only torch and full strict config from pyproject.toml
- Split typing dependencies into a dedicated group in `pyproject.toml`.
- Update `ci-typing.yml` to install only the typing group for type-checking.
- Remove `--ignore-missing-imports` from the pre-commit mypy hook configuration.

* Fix mypy typing issues across utility, dataset, inference, and training modules

commit.message:
fix(runtime): Capture execution failures in Sentry (#132)

Capture execution failures in Sentry

Junior currently logs `execution_failure` turns as warn-only
diagnostics, which is why the fallback response can show up in
production without a useful Sentry issue to inspect. This promotes those
turns to captured exceptions and attaches a short derived failure reason
to the event.

This is intentionally an observability-only change. It does not explain
or fix the underlying execution failure yet; it makes the next
production failure show up as actionable telemetry so we can investigate
it directly.

I also validated the linked `junior-prod` consumer with local `pnpm
build` runs in both repos.

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex GPT-5 <codex@openai.com>

chore(release): remove machine-specific windows test path

Summary:
- replace the tracked Administrator home path in the Windows runtime test
  fixture with a generic vault path
- keep the release safety preflight clear of machine-specific text while
  preserving the runtime-version assertions

Rationale:
- the local release dry-run correctly blocked on the tracked absolute path,
  so the repo needs a generic fixture before the release lane can proceed

Tests:
- node --test testing/native/device/windows/runtime-version.test.mjs
- rg -n "/Users/|/home/|[A-Za-z]:\\\\Users\\\\" --glob '!node_modules' \
  --glob '!dist' --glob '!coverage' .

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(chatview): preserve chronology and hosted continuations

Summary:
- keep reasoning, tool calls, and content in emitted order during live
  rendering and persisted reloads
- remap repeated hosted tool-call ids per assistant occurrence so managed
  continuations bind later tool results to the correct round
- add desktop automation coverage and autoresearch evidence for the
  saved-chat cutoff repro and provider-parity proof

Rationale:
- saved managed chats were collapsing chronology on reload and could end in
  blank assistant tool-use rounds with no final answer content
- the fix needs durable live repro helpers and regression locks so this lane
  stays verifiable instead of returning to ad hoc QA

Tests:
- bash autoresearch.sh
- AUTORESEARCH_RUN_ID=20260330T200230Z bash autoresearch.checks.sh
- node scripts/jest.mjs --config jest.config.cjs --runInBand \
  --runTestsByPath src/services/__tests__/SystemSculptService.test.ts
- node scripts/jest.mjs --config jest.config.cjs --runInBand \
  --runTestsByPath src/testing/automation/__tests__/DesktopAutomationBridge.test.ts
- node --test testing/native/desktop-automation/client.test.mjs
- node scripts/jest.mjs --config jest.config.cjs --runInBand \
  --runTestsByPath src/views/chatview/__tests__/input-handler-tool-loop.test.ts
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
chore(deps): consolidate Dependabot Go module minor upgrades (#1082)

### Motivation
- Consolidate multiple open Dependabot Go module minor updates into a
single rollup to reduce PR churn and follow the repository's
minor-upgrade guidance (`go get -t -u ./...`).
- Ensure the module `go` directive is updated where required so upgraded
dependencies that require a newer Go toolchain resolve correctly.

### Description
- Updated `go.mod` and `go.sum` to apply consolidated minor-version
upgrades for: `github.com/cli/cli/v2` (→ `v2.87.3`),
`github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go` (→ `v0.45.0`), the
`go.opentelemetry.io/otel` family (→ `v1.42.0`), `golang.org/x/sync` (→
`v0.20.0`), `golang.org/x/sys` (→ `v0.42.0`), `google.golang.org/api` (→
`v0.270.0`), `google.golang.org/grpc` (→ `v1.79.3`), and
`mvdan.cc/sh/v3` (→ `v3.13.0`).
- Updated the module `go` directive from `1.25.2` to `1.25.7` and
refreshed dependency checksums in `go.sum`.
- This PR consolidates the changes from Dependabot PRs: `#1077`,
`#1073`, `#1072`, `#1071`, `#1070`, `#1069`, `#1068`, `#1067`, `#1066`,
`#1065`, and `#1052`.

### Testing
- Ran `go get -t -u ./...` which downloaded and resolved the upgraded
modules and allowed `go` tool to switch to a compatible toolchain
version as required (succeeded for dependency resolution).
- Ran `go mod tidy` to prune and verify module graph (succeeded).
- Attempted `runme run lint test` but the `runme` CLI is not available
in this environment, so ran `make lint` and `make test` instead; `make
lint` failed in this environment because the `checklocks` vet tool is
not present, and `make test` executed but produced multiple failing
tests driven by environment/tooling/network assumptions (examples:
command package tests expecting `deno`/pty behavior, document/editor
tests expecting specific frontmatter/version behavior, and OIDC tests
requiring external network access), while many other packages' tests
passed.

------
[Codex
Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_69cab28c63c08324ad7ace6d59e2f0d6)

Signed-off-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Signed-off-by: Sebastian (Tiedtke) Huckleberry <sebastiantiedtke@gmail.com>

commit.message:
release(plugin): prepare 5.3.2 candidate

Summary:
- preserve completed local Pi tool-call state in ChatView so Pi tool
  executions do not get rerouted through the hosted tool loop
- pin Studio Pi auth inventory to the bundled auth-storage path and
  expand macOS and Windows provider-connected coverage on
  openai/gpt-5.4-mini
- harden Windows native release prep with runtime-version refresh,
  redacted bootstrap diagnostics, updated release notes, and 5.3.2
  version metadata

Rationale:
- keep canonical Pi auth and tool-calling paths strict instead of
  masking breakage behind fallbacks
- make the Windows release lane reproducible and future-agent friendly
  so stale runtimes and transient host noise stop causing trial and
  error
- capture the actual 5.3.2 release evidence in-tree before any publish
  step

Tests:
- node --test testing/native/desktop-automation/runner.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/bootstrap.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/runtime-version.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/run-desktop-automation.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/run-clean-install-parity.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/remote-run.test.mjs
- bash autoresearch.sh
- npm test
- npm run build
- npm run check:plugin
- SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_ID=openrouter
  SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_MODEL_ID=openai/gpt-5.4-mini
  SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_API_KEY="$OPENROUTER_API_KEY"
  npm run test:native:desktop:provider-connected
- SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_ID=openrouter
  SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_MODEL_ID=openai/gpt-5.4-mini
  SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_API_KEY="$OPENROUTER_API_KEY"
  npm run test:native:windows:provider-connected
- npm run check:release:native
- git diff --check
- gitleaks git . --log-opts='5.3.1..HEAD'

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Keep deploy docs anchored to the canonical guides

The README had grown into a second copy of the install and target-host
operator guides, which made the docs harder to keep aligned and blurred
the boundary between orchestration surfaces and the mutable ranctl path.
This change deletes repeated prose from the README, fixes one broken
examples link, and aligns the same boundary language across the docs
hub, the Symphony/Codex/skills architecture note, and the install /
real-server guides.

Constraint: Keep operator commands and proof boundaries unchanged
Rejected: Rewrite the guides into a new docs structure | unnecessary scope expansion for a deslop lane
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep detailed target-host dashboard exposure guidance canonical in 18 and prefer README pointers over duplicate walkthrough text
Tested: npm run docs:build; mise exec -- mix contract_ci; manual cross-link and proof-boundary review across README.md, docs/index.md, and architecture docs 06/12/16/17/18
Not-tested: Remote operator walkthrough execution on a live host
Related: YON-115
Related: YON-112

Co-authored-by: mud-the-developer <mud-the-developer@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: open-ran-agent
repository.description: Design-first open-source 5G SA RAN control and operations bootstrap with ranctl, deploy UX, and observability surfaces

commit.message:
Deslop ran_action_gateway and ran_config request/policy glue

Collapse the duplicated backend parsing in `RanActionGateway.Request`
and move policy description formatting into `RanConfig.ChangePolicy`
so the runner and config layer share one request/policy boundary.
The cleanup stays inside the selected file cluster and adds focused
regression tests for backend normalization and omitted-target policy
handling before reusing the formatter from the runner.

Constraint: Standard backends must still normalize to atoms while replacement backends stay as request strings
Rejected: Introduce a new shared glue module | adds another boundary for a small cleanup pass
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep request normalization and policy description changes paired with the focused request and policy tests
Tested: mix test apps/ran_action_gateway/test/ran_action_gateway/request_test.exs apps/ran_config/test/ran_config/change_policy_test.exs apps/ran_action_gateway/test/ran_action_gateway/cli_test.exs:220 apps/ran_action_gateway/test/ran_action_gateway/cli_test.exs:1112 apps/ran_action_gateway/test/ran_action_gateway/cli_test.exs:1134
Tested: mix compile --warnings-as-errors
Tested: mix contract_ci
Not-tested: mix runtime_ci

Co-authored-by: mud-the-developer <mud-the-developer@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: open-ran-agent
repository.description: Design-first open-source 5G SA RAN control and operations bootstrap with ranctl, deploy UX, and observability surfaces

commit.message:
Filter non-repo Docker containers out of Mission Control

The runtime board was still trusting broad `ran-*` name and compose
project prefixes after the first filtering pass. That let unrelated
stacks from other repositories survive whenever their container names
looked similar to the local OAI or agent lanes.

This change makes the compose working directory the primary ownership
signal by resolving it against the current repository's git common-dir.
Known `ran-oai` and `ran-agent-local` prefixes remain only as a narrow
fallback when Docker does not expose the workdir label. The snapshot
fixture now proves both sides of the contract: repo-local OAI and xApp
lanes stay visible, while foreign `ran-grafana` and `ran-agent-local`
compose workloads are dropped.

Constraint: Runtime board input is limited to `docker ps -a` metadata
Rejected: Keep broad `ran-*` matching | still leaks foreign compose
 stacks from other repos
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Treat compose working_dir as the primary ownership signal;
 only broaden prefix fallback after verifying a repo-owned lane lacks
 that label in production
Tested: `mix test apps/ran_observability/test/ran_observability/
 dashboard_snapshot_test.exs`
Tested: `mix run -e '...YON111ReproRunner...'`
Tested: `bin/ran-dashboard`; `curl http://127.0.0.1:4050/api/health`;
 `curl http://127.0.0.1:4050/api/dashboard`
Not-tested: Playwright PNG capture; local Chrome install required sudo

Co-authored-by: mud-the-developer <mud-the-developer@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(plugin): resolve reported UX regressions

Summary:
- track meeting processor outputs across renamed audio files so
  processed notes do not flip back to unprocessed
- restore the transcription clean-up prompt setting and prefer m4a
  recorder formats on mobile when supported
- stop forcing ribbon clustering, auto-create Studio projects on
  first open, and let chat hide system-role messages

Rationale:
- address the current GitHub and Discord complaint set with one
  coherent UX pass instead of partial follow-up commits
- keep the managed SystemSculpt flow intact while fixing missing
  controls and reducing intrusive UI behavior

Tests:
- npm run check:plugin
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(native): harden release gates and Pi auth

Summary:
- add a first-class native release matrix and wire combined macOS,
  Windows, and Android baseline commands into the plugin workflow
- default provider-connected desktop and Windows parity checks to the
  supported OpenRouter GPT-5.4 Mini path with clearer transient retry
  handling and baseline summaries
- align Pi auth storage, vault path resolution, Studio inventory, and
  desktop automation bridge behavior around the bundled auth backend

Rationale:
- keep release verification anchored to the native harness instead of
  ad hoc manual spot checks
- remove stale direct-OpenAI assumptions from the Windows provider
  lane so release prep uses the provider path that actually works here
- reduce auth and filesystem drift between plugin runtime code,
  Studio inspection, and native automation helpers

Tests:
- npm run check:plugin
- npm run check:release:native
- git diff --check
- gitleaks dir . --no-banner (hits were limited to ignored local files
  and existing build/cache artifacts, not the staged diff)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Improve activity labels for wrappers

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(workflow): keep merging issues routable and dashboards live

Summary:
- add `Merging` to active tracker states so post-review tickets keep
  polling until their PR merge is observed
- update merge handling to move already-merged PRs directly to `Done`
  instead of treating them like stale branch resets
- tighten project overview instructions so the Linear description stays
  a live PR review priority dashboard instead of drifting into a stale
  narrative summary

Rationale:
- StackPerf had several merged issues stranded in `Merging` because the
  workflow stopped polling them after the human state transition
- the live dashboard only helps reviewers if agents keep regenerating it
  in place as queue conditions change

Tests:
- not run (workflow prompt update only)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
